description_long: This cowshed features a large, rectangular structure with a prominent hip roof covered in gray shingles, providing ample shelter. The main building is solid and windowless, with dark exterior walls that contrast with the lighter roof. Attached to one side is an open, wooden-framed extension, likely serving as a sheltered outdoor area or feeding space for livestock. The wooden extension is constructed with visible beams and posts, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. No doors or windows are visible, emphasizing a simple, functional design. The overall form and materials suggest durability and practicality, suitable for agricultural use in an urban or peri-urban setting.
description: A large, hip-roofed cowshed with dark walls and a wooden-framed side extension, designed for practical agricultural use.
description_view_0: The main hip roof dominates, with the wooden-framed extension clearly visible on the left side.
description_view_1: The hip roof is seen from the side, with the wooden extension now at the far left, showing its open framework.
description_view_2: A direct view of the solid, windowless wall and the full expanse of the hip roof, with minimal extension visible.
description_view_3: The wooden-framed extension is now on the right, with the main building's side wall and roof in full view.
